verbGlossary.TRANSITIONS TO: {target} moves to the next state in the lifecycle sequence when the required action or condition is met; represents the standard forward progression through a workflow
verbGlossary.ADVANCES TO: A pending or waiting state progresses to an active evaluation state when the responsible party begins their review; represents the start of deliberate action on a queued item
verbGlossary.RESOLVES AS: A review or evaluation state resolves into a terminal decision or disposition; represents the outcome of deliberation by the responsible party
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source}initiates a corrective {target} that sends the record back for revision; represents the start of a rework cycle within the approval process
verbGlossary.CYCLES BACK TO: A revision or rework state returns the record to a previous submission point for another pass through the review {target}; represents the loop in iterative approval
verbGlossary.CANCELS INTO: {source} is cancelled and moved to a void state when the work is abandoned or invalidated; represents permanent {target} termination without completion
verbGlossary.PAUSES INTO: {source} is suspended into a hold state when an external dependency or decision blocks further progress; represents a temporary {target} stall
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS WHEN EXCEEDED: A date-based threshold {target}s a computed status when the current date passes the deadline without the expected state transition; represents an automatic escalation condition
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: One {target} event or condition directly causes another workflow action to fire; represents a cause-and-effect chain within the process
verbGlossary.MAY INVOKE: {source}may optionally invoke a delegation or reassignment when the original responsible party remains unresponsive; represents a conditional fallback {target}
verbGlossary.REASSIGNS: A delegation action transfers responsibility and updates {target} to a new party; represents a change of accountability within the workflow
verbGlossary.COMPOSED OF: A multi-step {target} chain is composed of individual workflow steps arranged in sequence or parallel; represents the structural decomposition of a review process
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: {target} produces a formal response record when the assigned party takes action; represents the output of a review decision at a single stage
verbGlossary.NOTIFIED BY: {source}receives notifications when {target} response is recorded; represents the information broadcast that keeps stakeholders informed without requiring their action
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {target} pattern defines the structural arrangement of an approval chain; represents how reviewers are organized sequentially or in parallel within the process
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINED BY: {target} pattern is constrained by a time-bound requirement that governs how quickly the responsible party must respond; represents the contractual pacing control on review cycles
verbGlossary.BEGINS WITH: {target} pattern starts with a specific status as its entry point; represents the initial state that kicks off the pattern's lifecycle
verbGlossary.TERMINATES AT: {target} pattern ends at a specific terminal status; represents the final state that closes out the pattern's lifecycle
verbGlossary.REOPENS AS: A deficient or failed disposition reopens the record as an active item requiring corrective action; represents the rework {target} from field inspection findings
verbGlossary.EXCLUDES VIA: {source}determines a record or checklist item is irrelevant and removes it from the active {target} via a not-applicable disposition; represents scope exclusion during field review
verbGlossary.DECOMPOSES INTO: A multi-stage financial {target} decomposes into independent approval stages that each track their own status; represents the parallel financial review structure
verbGlossary.GOVERNED BY: {source}is governed by the multi-stage financial review pattern that defines its progression; represents the pattern that controls the composite {target}
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: One financial {target} stage must reach approval before the next stage can proceed; represents the sequencing of budget, cost, and revenue decisions
verbGlossary.NORMALIZES: A mapped {target} translates domain-specific status values into a common vocabulary for cross-record comparison; represents the standardization layer across different workflow types
verbGlossary.OVERLAYS: {target} request runs as a parallel workflow that overlays the standard approval process; represents an independent but synchronized signing track
verbGlossary.MIRRORS: The e-signature {target} mirrors the construction ball-in-court concept within the digital signing workflow; represents the parallel accountability tracking for document execution
verbGlossary.MEASURES EFFICIENCY OF: {source}measure calculates the time efficiency of {target} chain by averaging cycle times across submissions; represents a workflow performance benchmark
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source}measure quantifies the frequency or magnitude of a specific {target} condition; represents a count-based metric for process monitoring
verbGlossary.COMPLEMENTS: {source}measures are mathematical complements that together account for the full outcome space; one measures success rate while the other measures the corresponding completion benchmark
verbGlossary.MEASURES RATE OF: {source}measure calculates the percentage of records reaching a specific outcome; represents a ratio metric for {target} quality assessment
verbGlossary.MEASURES DWELL OF: {source}measure calculates how long responsibility sits with the current party before action; represents an accountability metric for {target} responsiveness
verbGlossary.MEASURES DURATION OF: {source}measure calculates the average time spent in each {target}; represents a bottleneck identification metric across process stages
verbGlossary.MEASURES FREQUENCY OF: {source}measure calculates how often a specific {target} event occurs relative to total actionable items; represents an exception rate metric for process friction
